title: A New Creation in Christ
story:
I recall a friend who struggled with addiction and felt trapped by his circumstances.

But after coming to faith in Christ, he experienced a radical transformation. He began attending church regularly, read Scripture daily, and started serving others.

Over time, the chains of addiction broke loose, and he emerged as a new creation — full of hope, joy, and purpose.
life_lesson:
The old self, with its sinful nature, is like a worn-out garment — torn and frayed by the trials of this world.

But when we put on Christ, we are clothed in His righteousness, making us fit for eternity.

We are not just trying to become better versions of ourselves; we are being remade into the very image of God.
prayer:
Dear Heavenly Father,

We thank You for being the Potter who remakes us according to Your divine plan. Help us to see ourselves as new creations in Christ, freed from the chains of our old self.

Give us the courage to let go of what no longer belongs in our lives and trust that You are leading us into a brighter future.

As we walk in this new life, empower us with Your Holy Spirit to live out our faith and spread hope and love wherever we go.

In Jesus' name we pray,
Amen.
reflection_question:
In what ways do you need to put off your old self and put on Christ today?

Are there relationships or situations where you are struggling to let go of the past?

Remember that becoming a new creation is not just about removing our flaws, but also about growing into all that God has created us to be.
verse: If anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation; the old has gone, the new has come!
